arrivals.title: The Disinterested Witness: A Fragment of Advaita Vedanta Phenomenology
arrivals.author: Gupta, Bina
arrivals.description: A philosophical exploration of the concept of the 'witness-self' (sākṣin) in Advaita Vedānta, a principal school of Indian philosophy. This book analyzes the nature and existence of the witness-consciousness, which refers to the self that remains a passive observer, distinct from the body, mind, and senses. It explores how this inner witness underlies all cognition and awareness while itself remaining unaffected.

arrivals.title: Hooked
arrivals.author: Yuzuki, Asako
arrivals.description: A novel that explores themes of obsession, desire, and the complexities of modern relationships. The story centers around a woman who becomes intensely fixated on another person, leading her down a path of emotional turmoil and self-discovery. Yuzuki delves into the psychological depths of her characters, unpacking the fine lines between love, longing, and unhealthy attachment.

arrivals.title: The Groundbreaker: Risks, Rewards, and Lessons from a Legendary Entrepreneur
arrivals.author: Rekhi, Kanwal
arrivals.description: A memoir and business book that chronicles the remarkable entrepreneurial journey of Kanwal Rekhi, a legendary entrepreneur and investor. The book explores the risks taken, rewards earned, and valuable lessons learned throughout his career in building successful ventures and contributing to India's technology and business landscape.

arrivals.title: Every Last Girl: A Journey to Educate India's Forgotten Daughters
arrivals.author: Husain, Safeena
arrivals.description: An inspiring account of Safeena Husain's work to fight for girls' education in India, particularly focusing on forgotten and marginalized daughters. The book chronicles her journey in establishing the Educate Girls organization and the transformative impact of providing education to girls in rural and underserved communities across India.

arrivals.title: The Broken Rung: When the Career Ladder Breaks for Women and How They Can Succeed in Spite of It
arrivals.author: Ellingrud, Kweilin
arrivals.description: A business book that addresses the challenges women face in advancing their careers. The title metaphorically refers to the career ladder with a broken rung—representing the barriers women encounter. The book offers practical strategies and insights from McKinsey research on how women can overcome these obstacles and succeed in their professional journeys.

arrivals.title: India Innovates: Technological Sovereignty in a Weaponized World
arrivals.author: Sharma, Pranjal
arrivals.description: A contemporary analysis of India's technological development and innovation landscape in the context of global geopolitical challenges. The book examines how India can achieve technological sovereignty and independence in an increasingly weaponized global environment, exploring India's potential as a technological innovator and leader.

arrivals.title: Inspire: The Universal Path for Leading Yourself and Others
arrivals.author: Galinsky, Adam
arrivals.description: A science-based book exploring the psychology behind motivation and influence. Co-authored with Loran Nordgren, it introduces the concept of engineering inspiration by breaking down barriers to action. The book draws on decades of social science research and practical advice to explain how anyone can unlock motivation in themselves and others.

arrivals.title: Lights Out, Minds On: The Psychology Of Speed and the Human Stories Behind Formula 1
arrivals.author: Awasthi, Priyanka
arrivals.description: An engaging exploration of the psychology of speed and the human stories behind Formula 1 racing. The book delves into the mental aspects of high-performance racing, the drivers' experiences, and the human drama that unfolds in one of the world's most competitive motorsports.

arrivals.title: The Midnight Library
arrivals.author: Haig, Matt
arrivals.description: A bestselling novel that explores themes of regret, hope, and the possibilities that exist between life and death. Nora Seed finds herself in the mysterious Midnight Library, where each book represents a different path her life could have taken. Through exploring these alternate realities, she gains insight into her own regrets and what truly matters to her.

arrivals.title: World's Best Girlfriend
arrivals.author: Datta, Durjoy
arrivals.description: A contemporary romance novel set in modern India exploring themes of love, ambition, rivalry, and self-discovery. The story revolves around two main characters, Arush and Vibha, whose lives intersect at a prestigious college as they vie for academic excellence and recognition, discovering the complexities of competition and connection.

arrivals.title: 12 Years: My Messed-up Love Story
arrivals.author: Bhagat, Chetan
arrivals.description: A contemporary Indian romance novel by bestselling author Chetan Bhagat that explores the complexities, ups and downs, and the journey of a twelve-year romantic relationship. The book delves into real-life challenges, emotional turbulence, and the growth that comes from a long-term love story.

arrivals.title: Prescription for Excellence: Leadership Lessons for Creating a World-Class Customer Experience from UCLA Health System
arrivals.author: Michelli, Joseph A
arrivals.description: A business and leadership book that explores the management strategies and organizational culture that made UCLA Health System one of the top healthcare providers in the United States. The book highlights how leaders place patient care, teamwork, and integrity at the forefront of every decision while achieving operational excellence.

arrivals.title: Joyful, Anyway
arrivals.author: Bowler, Kate
arrivals.description: An inspirational book about finding joy and meaning despite life's challenges and hardships. Kate Bowler reflects on her personal experiences and offers readers wisdom on how to cultivate joy in any circumstance, emphasizing resilience and the power of choosing gratitude.

arrivals.title: River Stories
arrivals.author: Hughes, Henry
arrivals.description: A collection of short stories that delve into the lives and landscapes shaped by rivers. The stories blend literary prose with natural observation, exploring themes of human relationships, nature, memory, and change, set against the backdrop of rivers both real and imagined.

arrivals.title: Agents of Change: Strategy and Tactics for Social Innovation
arrivals.author: Cels, Sanderijn
arrivals.description: A comprehensive guide to understanding and implementing social innovation. The book explores strategy and tactics for creating positive social change, offering frameworks and case studies for individuals and organizations looking to make a meaningful impact on society.

arrivals.title: Careless People: A Story of Where I Used to Work
arrivals.author: Wynn-Williams, Sarah
arrivals.description: A work memoir that explores the experiences and dynamics of a workplace where negligence and carelessness contributed to a particular incident or culture. The book reflects on corporate environments and personal experiences within them, offering insights into workplace behavior and organizational culture.

arrivals.title: The Way Back Home
arrivals.author: Jeffers, Oliver
arrivals.description: A beautifully illustrated children's picture book about a little boy who discovers a single-propeller airplane in his closet and flies to the moon. There he meets a stranded Martian, and together they work to solve their problems and find their way back home—a heartwarming story about friendship and teamwork.

arrivals.title: Alchemy: The Surprising Power of Ideas That Don't Make Sense
arrivals.author: Sutherland, Rory
arrivals.description: An exploration of the irrational nature of human behavior and decision-making. Advertising legend Rory Sutherland argues that the most successful ideas often defy logic, and that embracing illogic and curious experimentation can lead to real innovation in business and life.

arrivals.title: The School of Night
arrivals.author: Knausgaard, Karl Ove
arrivals.description: A literary work exploring themes of education, identity, and human connection. The novel delves into the complexities of schooling and personal relationships, written in the introspective style characteristic of Karl Ove Knausgaard's narrative approach.

arrivals.title: Animalia
arrivals.author: Base, Graeme
arrivals.description: A classic children's alphabet picture book with lavishly illustrated pages for each letter A-Z. Each page features intricate artwork with animals and objects corresponding to that letter, written in playful, alliterative verse. The book serves as a visual puzzle and language introduction for children.

arrivals.title: If I Built a House
arrivals.author: Van Dusen, Chris
arrivals.description: A vibrant, imaginative children's picture book where a young boy named Jack dreams up his perfect house with creative features like a racetrack, flying rooms, giant slides, and underwater aquarium rooms. The story encourages creativity and thinking outside the box with bright, energetic illustrations.

arrivals.title: If I Built a Car
arrivals.author: Van Dusen, Chris
arrivals.description: A vibrant picture book where young Jack dreams up the ultimate car of the future with amazing features including a snack bar, pool, robotic driver, and wings for flying. The book celebrates imagination and invention with colorful, retro-inspired illustrations and rhyming text.

arrivals.title: Outside In
arrivals.author: Underwood, Deborah
arrivals.description: A beautifully illustrated picture book that explores the vital connections between the outside world and our everyday lives. Through poetic text and evocative art, the book invites readers to appreciate how nature influences us, even when we're indoors, celebrating the wonders of nature's quiet, constant presence.

arrivals.title: Sea: A World Beneath the Waves
arrivals.author: Hegarty, Patricia
arrivals.description: A beautifully illustrated children's book that explores the amazing world beneath the ocean's surface. With poetic text and captivating illustrations, the book takes readers on a journey through the ocean's different layers, highlighting the diversity and wonder of sea life with interactive cut-out sections.

arrivals.title: Diary of a Worm
arrivals.author: Cronin, Doreen
arrivals.description: A charming and humorous children's book written in diary format by a young worm. Through witty daily entries, the worm shares what life is like underground and above. The book introduces children to ecological ideas while delivering lessons about acceptance, friendship, and the importance of every creature.

arrivals.title: Diary of a Fly
arrivals.author: Cronin, Doreen
arrivals.description: A humorous children's picture book in diary format about a young fly's life. The clever fly records her daily adventures, thoughts, and challenges as she grows up. Through witty entries and delightful illustrations, readers learn interesting facts about flies and see their world from a unique insect perspective.

arrivals.title: Diary of a Spider
arrivals.author: Cronin, Doreen
arrivals.description: A humorous children's picture book told through the diary of a young spider. The spider writes about his family, school adventures, and friendships, providing a witty spider's-eye view of the world. The book encourages readers to appreciate different perspectives and see things from an insect's point of view.

arrivals.title: Butter
arrivals.author: Yuzuki, Asako
arrivals.description: A Japanese novel that weaves together themes of food, femininity, loneliness, and desire. The story follows Rika, a journalist investigating a woman accused of seducing and possibly murdering wealthy men with her extraordinary butter-laden dishes. The narrative explores societal pressures on women, body image, and food as both weapon and source of fulfillment.

arrivals.title: The Lost Stories
arrivals.author: Flanagan, John
arrivals.description: The eleventh book in the Ranger's Apprentice series, this is a collection of short stories that fills gaps in the main storyline. Set within the world of Araluen, stories reveal backstories and answer fan questions about main characters like Will, Halt, and others. The stories are framed by an archaeological dig that uncovers lost manuscripts.

arrivals.title: The Founder Manual: Insights from the Top Founders of India
arrivals.author: Somani, Utsav
arrivals.description: A comprehensive guide offering insights and lessons from India's most successful startup founders. The book covers entrepreneurial strategies, validation of business ideas, and practical wisdom from some of India's most accomplished entrepreneurs and innovators.

arrivals.title: Startups of Bharat: Stories of India's Million-Dollar Founders Under Thirty
arrivals.author: Arora, Aditya
arrivals.description: A collection of inspiring stories from India's entrepreneurial landscape, focusing on innovative startups and founders under thirty who have built million-dollar businesses. The book explores the unique challenges and opportunities in building startups in non-metro regions of India.

arrivals.title: The Little Book Of Indian Business
arrivals.author: Finshots
arrivals.description: A concise guide to understanding Indian business fundamentals and concepts. Written by Finshots, this little book breaks down complex business topics relevant to India's economy and entrepreneurs in an accessible and engaging manner.

arrivals.title: If I Built a School
arrivals.author: Van Dusen, Chris
arrivals.description: A fun and imaginative children's picture book where a young boy named Jack dreams up his ideal school with futuristic features like hover desks, sky-high playgrounds, robo-chefs, and glass tube classrooms. The rhyming verses celebrate a place where learning and fun go hand-in-hand.

arrivals.title: Unravelled
arrivals.author: Messenger, Shannon
arrivals.description: A book in the Keeper of the Lost Cities series by Shannon Messenger. This installment continues the adventures of Sophie and her friends in the hidden fantasy world, with new mysteries to unravel and challenges to overcome.

arrivals.title: Organizational Learning from Performance Feedback: A Behavioral Perspective on Multiple Goals
arrivals.author: Audia, Pino G.
arrivals.description: A detailed examination of how organizations learn and adapt in response to their performance experiences. The book explores performance feedback theory, analyzing how organizations adjust behavior based on evaluations of their performance relative to goals and aspirations.

arrivals.title: Professional Real Estate Development: The ULI Guide to the Business
arrivals.author: Peiser, Richard B.
arrivals.description: A comprehensive textbook and reference guide covering the entire real estate development process from conception to marketing. Now in its third edition, it provides systematic, step-by-step guidance with practical examples for developers, investors, planners, and students.

arrivals.title: Data Analysis for Business, Economics, and Policy
arrivals.author: Békés, Gábor
arrivals.description: A comprehensive textbook offering practical data analysis skills for economics, business, and public policy fields. The book provides hands-on guidance using real-world data and modern software (R and Stata) to interpret and communicate results effectively for business and policy professionals.
